Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.asset

Interface FlixelAsset<T>

Type Parameters:
T - LibGDX-loaded asset type (e.g. Texture).
All Known Implementing Classes:
FlixelGraphic, FlixelSound, FlixelTypedAsset
public interface FlixelAsset<T>
Typed handle for one asset path + class, with optional persist and refcount.

Obtain pooled handles with FlixelAssetManager.obtainTypedAsset(String, Class) (cached on Flixel.assets). FlixelGraphic and FlixelSound also implement this contract where applicable.

Prefer queueLoad() in a loading state and Flixel.assets.update() each frame.

    Modifier and Type

    Description
    @NotNull String
    getAssetKey()

    int
    getRefCount()
    Gets and returns the reference count of this asset.
    @NotNull Class<T>
    getType()
    Gets and returns the type of the asset.
    boolean
    isPersist()
    Checks if this asset will be kept in memory after the game state is switched.
    T
    loadNow()
    Explicit synchronous load for one-off cases.
    void
    queueLoad()
    Enqueues this asset into the active FlixelAssetManager.

    release()
    Decrements the reference count of this asset.
    T
    require()
    Requires the asset to already be loaded.
    @NotNull FlixelAsset<T>
    retain()
    Increments the reference count of this asset.
    @NotNull FlixelAsset<T>
    setPersist(boolean persist)
    Sets whether this asset will be kept in memory after the game state is switched.

  • Method Details

    • getAssetKey

      @NotNull @NotNull String getAssetKey()
      Gets and returns the asset key associated with this asset.
      Returns:
      The asset key.

    • getType

      @NotNull @NotNull Class<T> getType()
      Gets and returns the type of the asset.
      Returns:
      The asset type.

    • isPersist

      boolean isPersist()
      Checks if this asset will be kept in memory after the game state is switched.
      Returns:
      true if the asset will be kept in memory after the game state is switched, false otherwise.

    • setPersist

      @NotNull @NotNull FlixelAsset<T> setPersist(boolean persist)
      Sets whether this asset will be kept in memory after the game state is switched.
      Parameters:
      persist - true if the asset will be kept in memory after the game state is switched, false otherwise.
      Returns:
      this asset for chaining.

    • getRefCount

      int getRefCount()
      Gets and returns the reference count of this asset.
      Returns:
      The reference count.

    • retain

      @NotNull @NotNull FlixelAsset<T> retain()
      Increments the reference count of this asset.
      Returns:
      this asset for chaining.

    • release

      @NotNull @NotNull FlixelAsset<T> release()
      Decrements the reference count of this asset.
      Returns:
      this asset for chaining.

    • queueLoad

      void queueLoad()
      Enqueues this asset into the active FlixelAssetManager. Safe to call multiple times.

      Use this method to preload assets in your game's loading state.

    • require

      @NotNull T require()
      Requires the asset to already be loaded.
      Returns:
      The loaded asset.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the asset is not loaded.

    • loadNow

      @NotNull T loadNow()
      Explicit synchronous load for one-off cases. Avoid using implicitly during gameplay.
